name: Bedford Road Medical (Friendly PC) › DBA for Doro Medical
notes:
Now that we're getting our friendly PC off the ground and identifiers assigned, could we get that DBA in?

Here's Cooley's take on DBAs for the PC generally: 

On the DBA, New York requires the DBA to meet the same naming restrictions as the original name, so “Doro” could not be used as part of a DBA. See NYSED “Please be aware that assumed names and dbas must still meet the same naming requirements as original names.”

Telehealth clients that provide services through a “friendly PC” arrangement generally license the MSO’s name to the PC(s). The MSO’s name is used by the PC as the publicly facing brand. The NY PC’s official name is “Bedford Road Medical”, however,  we could use the Doro MSO’s name in publicly facing material through the license granted by the MSO under the management services agreement or another type of licensing agreement. This concept is baked into the Management Agreement we will send.

For example, telehealth company Hims also uses a friendly PC model. The clinical services are provided by their PCs, which do not use the “Hims” name. Instead, the PCs include the following: You Health of Alaska, P.C., You Health of California P.C., P.A., You Health of Hawaii, P.C., You Health of Kansas, P.A., You Health of Nebraska, P.C., You Health Williams of Nevada, P.C., You Health of New Jersey, P.C., and You Health of Texas, P.A. To the public, the PCs are all part of the same “Hims” umbrella. The PCs’ names are generally only included in the terms of use and other legal documents.


And then the NYSED rules:

5. Unless otherwise authorized by law, your application will be rejected if your entity name does not contain the profession being practiced. Regulations of the Commissioner of Education, https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/www.op.nysed.gov/title8/regulations-commissioner-education/part-59*psc__;Iw!!OPvj_Mo!_zGULyJ8rhSEbEkMVz5cveyfPtMBF7HChCPjYjrTTZ6Ub0SLMKmPCYZ51s4kgCmpJum8LMNfPsC4Olk$.
 
7. Your application will be rejected if you do not submit an explanation for words used in your entity name that are: initials, foreign words, surnames, abbreviations or words not commonly used in connection with the profession(s) being practiced (https://urldefense.com/v3/__http:/public.leginfo.state.ny.us/LAWSSEAF.cgi?QUERYTYPE=LAWS*&QUERYDATA=$$BSC1503$$@TXBSC01503*&LIST=LAW*&BROWSER=EXPLORER*&TOKEN=27738319*&TARGET=VIEW__;KysrKys!!OPvj_Mo!_zGULyJ8rhSEbEkMVz5cveyfPtMBF7HChCPjYjrTTZ6Ub0SLMKmPCYZ51s4kgCmpJum8LMNfMwheFmU$; https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/www.op.nysed.gov/title8/regulations-commissioner-education/part-59*psc__;Iw!!OPvj_Mo!_zGULyJ8rhSEbEkMVz5cveyfPtMBF7HChCPjYjrTTZ6Ub0SLMKmPCYZ51s4kgCmpJum8LMNfPsC4Olk$; https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/www.op.nysed.gov/title8/rules-board-regents/part-29__;!!OPvj_Mo!_zGULyJ8rhSEbEkMVz5cveyfPtMBF7HChCPjYjrTTZ6Ub0SLMKmPCYZ51s4kgCmpJum8LMNfKf3srB8$). […]
 
14. Your application will be rejected if the name of the entity includes a term that does not meet the requirements set forth in https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/www.counsel.nysed.gov/pamphlet9/__;!!OPvj_Mo!_zGULyJ8rhSEbEkMVz5cveyfPtMBF7HChCPjYjrTTZ6Ub0SLMKmPCYZ51s4kgCmpJum8LMNff4pMbPw$.

*as per Article 28 of the Public Health Law

name: Claude BAA
notes:
Short summary of next steps: 
    Get an org API key. We use that to authenticate instead of Claude Teams
    Maria sets up zero data retention (ZDR)
    We sign a BAA

Gist of the conversation is was that they want this to be easier, and are working to make it easier. Right now there are some parts of their system which are easy to cover with a BAA, and parts that take more manual work on their side, which are the ones that need an enterprise account to get BAA coverage. Maria hinted that that might change quite soon, but didn't give a date. 

What was in vs out was a bit hard to follow. Maria said she'd send it in a follow-up email. What was clear was that Claude Cowork and Claude Code UI were hard, while API and CLI Claude Code, authenticated with an API key, were easy.

name: Note on the family-facing experience at Doro Mind
notes:
https://mail.google.com/mail/#inbox/19e0c2bea0a707cd

From: David Balinga <hi@davidbalinga.com>
To: hello@doromind.com

Hi,

I work on UX specifically for cognitively and emotionally stretched users.
Families navigating serious mental illness, schizophrenia, bipolar
disorder, are one of the most cognitively and emotionally loaded user
populations there is. They arrive at products like Doro Mind mid-crisis,
often sleep-deprived, guilt-ridden, and making decisions they’ve never had
to make before.

That user state makes the family-facing experience one of the hardest
design problems in mental health tech, because standard UX patterns assume
a user with working executive function. Families in this situation often
don’t have it, and the gap usually shows up as silent dropoff rather than
complaints.

If reducing friction in the family-facing experience is something the team
is thinking about, I’d be happy to put together a longer async writeup on
what I notice across the product.

David / davidbalinga.com

the rejection reason is:
```Upstream CNP declined sharing request for campaign C7ULFSF. Rejection Category: DISALLOWED_CONTENT, CALL_TO_ACTION. Explanation: Disallowed Content (Third Party Data Sharing). Call to Action states Jot form is used to collect opt-in, Webform requires TN; update CTA/missing disclaimer (msg frequency, msg and data rates may apply, and HELP/STOP instructions are missing from webform disclosures. IB```
i asked Gemini to help me parse this and it says we need to:
1. Update our privacy policy to say _"Mobile information will not be shared with third parties/affiliates for marketing/promotional purposes. All the above categories exclude text messaging originator opt-in data and consent; this information will not be shared with any third parties."_
2. Update the jotform where we collect phone numbers with three pieces of info: msg frequency, "msg and data rates may apply", and HELP/STOP instructions
after we do those, i can resubmit.
